title: coriaria lurida var. undulata
intro: Coriaria lurida var. undulata, also known as the New Zealand pepper tree, is a species of flowering plant that is native to New Zealand and parts of the South Island. It is used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including its ability to treat rheumatism, fever, and skin conditions. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing pain and promoting wound healing. Coriaria lurida var. undulata can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as tinctures, infusions, and salves, which are often used to treat various health issues.
